digest.component_or_area: title validation in composer command (CLI/library/MCP facades)
propositions: Whitespace-only titles are wrongly accepted because validation uses `if not title:` instead of checking `title.strip()`; they must be rejected on create and edit across CLI, library, and MCP.
A title with surrounding whitespace but real content must be accepted and stored byte-identically without silent trimming.
Create-path tests are missing for empty and >255-character title rejection, which currently has code enforcement but only edit-path test coverage.

A curated cross-facade text corpus (leading dashes, quotes, backslashes, LF/CRLF, emoji/non-BMP, NFC/NFD forms as separate cases, and a >=1000-char string) must round-trip byte-identically or be rejected identically across all three facades.
Unicode is preserved byte-identically with the one documented exception that U+2192 is substituted to `->`; NFC and NFD forms are treated as distinct per-form values.
This is a user-visible tightening requiring a back-out/expand-contract note, a pre-merge store scan for existing whitespace-only titles, and updated CHANGELOG and CLI help text.
